root_text.an4.180:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā bhoganagare viharati ānandacetiye.
root_text.an4.180:1.7: taṁ suṇātha, sādhukaṁ manasi karotha, bhāsissāmī”ti.
root_text.an4.180:1.8: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho te bhikkhū bhagavato paccassosuṁ.
root_text.an4.180:2.3: ‘sammukhā metaṁ, āvuso, bhagavato sutaṁ sammukhā paṭiggahitaṁ—
root_text.an4.180:2.4: ayaṁ dhammo, ayaṁ vinayo, idaṁ satthusāsanan’ti.
root_text.an4.180:2.5: Tassa, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno bhāsitaṁ neva abhinanditabbaṁ nappaṭikkositabbaṁ.
root_text.an4.180:2.6: Anabhinanditvā appaṭikkositvā tāni padabyañjanāni sādhukaṁ uggahetvā sutte otāretabbāni, vinaye sandassetabbāni.
root_text.an4.180:2.7: Tāni ce sutte otāriyamānāni vinaye sandassiyamānāni na ceva sutte otaranti na vinaye sandissanti, niṭṭhamettha gantabbaṁ:
root_text.an4.180:2.8: ‘addhā idaṁ na ceva tassa bhagavato vacanaṁ arahato sammāsambuddhassa;
root_text.an4.180:3.1: Idha pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhu evaṁ vadeyya:
root_text.an4.180:3.2: ‘sammukhā metaṁ, āvuso, bhagavato sutaṁ sammukhā paṭiggahitaṁ—
root_text.an4.180:3.3: ayaṁ dhammo, ayaṁ vinayo, idaṁ satthusāsanan’ti.
root_text.an4.180:3.4: Tassa, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno bhāsitaṁ neva abhinanditabbaṁ nappaṭikkositabbaṁ.
root_text.an4.180:3.5: Anabhinanditvā appaṭikkositvā tāni padabyañjanāni sādhukaṁ uggahetvā sutte otāretabbāni, vinaye sandassetabbāni.
root_text.an4.180:3.6: Tāni ce sutte otāriyamānāni vinaye sandassiyamānāni sutte ceva otaranti vinaye ca sandissanti, niṭṭhamettha gantabbaṁ:
root_text.an4.180:3.7: ‘addhā idaṁ tassa bhagavato vacanaṁ arahato sammāsambuddhassa;
root_text.an4.180:4.1: Idha pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhu evaṁ vadeyya:
root_text.an4.180:4.2: ‘asukasmiṁ nāma āvāse saṅgho viharati sathero sapāmokkho.
root_text.an4.180:4.3: Tassa me saṅghassa sammukhā sutaṁ sammukhā paṭiggahitaṁ—
root_text.an4.180:4.4: ayaṁ dhammo, ayaṁ vinayo, idaṁ satthusāsanan’ti.
root_text.an4.180:4.5: Tassa, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno bhāsitaṁ neva abhinanditabbaṁ nappaṭikkositabbaṁ.
root_text.an4.180:4.6: Anabhinanditvā appaṭikkositvā tāni padabyañjanāni sādhukaṁ uggahetvā sutte otāretabbāni, vinaye sandassetabbāni.
root_text.an4.180:4.7: Tāni ce sutte otāriyamānāni vinaye sandassiyamānāni na ceva sutte otaranti na vinaye sandissanti, niṭṭhamettha gantabbaṁ:
root_text.an4.180:4.8: ‘addhā idaṁ na ceva tassa bhagavato vacanaṁ arahato sammāsambuddhassa;
root_text.an4.180:5.1: Idha pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhu evaṁ vadeyya:
root_text.an4.180:5.2: ‘asukasmiṁ nāma āvāse saṅgho viharati sathero sapāmokkho.
root_text.an4.180:5.3: Tassa me saṅghassa sammukhā sutaṁ sammukhā paṭiggahitaṁ—
root_text.an4.180:5.4: ayaṁ dhammo, ayaṁ vinayo, idaṁ satthusāsanan’ti.
root_text.an4.180:5.5: Tassa, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno bhāsitaṁ neva abhinanditabbaṁ nappaṭikkositabbaṁ.
root_text.an4.180:5.6: Anabhinanditvā appaṭikkositvā tāni padabyañjanāni sādhukaṁ uggahetvā sutte otāretabbāni, vinaye sandassetabbāni.
root_text.an4.180:5.7: Tāni ce sutte otāriyamānāni, vinaye sandassiyamānāni sutte ceva otaranti vinaye ca sandissanti, niṭṭhamettha gantabbaṁ:
root_text.an4.180:5.8: ‘addhā idaṁ tassa bhagavato vacanaṁ arahato sammāsambuddhassa;
root_text.an4.180:6.1: Idha pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhu evaṁ vadeyya:
root_text.an4.180:6.2: ‘asukasmiṁ nāma āvāse sambahulā therā bhikkhū viharanti bahussutā āgatāgamā dhammadharā vinayadharā mātikādharā.
root_text.an4.180:6.3: Tesaṁ me therānaṁ sammukhā sutaṁ sammukhā paṭiggahitaṁ—
root_text.an4.180:6.4: ayaṁ dhammo, ayaṁ vinayo, idaṁ satthusāsanan’ti.
root_text.an4.180:6.5: Tassa, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno bhāsitaṁ neva abhinanditabbaṁ nappaṭikkositabbaṁ.
root_text.an4.180:6.6: Anabhinanditvā appaṭikkositvā tāni padabyañjanāni sādhukaṁ uggahetvā sutte otāretabbāni, vinaye sandassetabbāni.
root_text.an4.180:6.7: Tāni ce sutte otāriyamānāni vinaye sandassiyamānāni na ceva sutte otaranti na vinaye sandissanti, niṭṭhamettha gantabbaṁ:
root_text.an4.180:6.8: ‘addhā idaṁ na ceva tassa bhagavato vacanaṁ arahato sammāsambuddhassa;
root_text.an4.180:7.1: Idha pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhu evaṁ vadeyya:
root_text.an4.180:7.2: ‘asukasmiṁ nāma āvāse sambahulā therā bhikkhū viharanti bahussutā āgatāgamā dhammadharā vinayadharā mātikādharā.
root_text.an4.180:7.3: Tesaṁ me therānaṁ sammukhā sutaṁ sammukhā paṭiggahitaṁ—
root_text.an4.180:7.4: ayaṁ dhammo, ayaṁ vinayo, idaṁ satthusāsanan’ti.
root_text.an4.180:7.5: Tassa, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno bhāsitaṁ neva abhinanditabbaṁ nappaṭikkositabbaṁ.
root_text.an4.180:7.6: Anabhinanditvā appaṭikkositvā tāni padabyañjanāni sādhukaṁ uggahetvā sutte otāretabbāni, vinaye sandassetabbāni.
root_text.an4.180:7.7: Tāni ce sutte otāriyamānāni vinaye sandassiyamānāni sutte ceva otaranti vinaye ca sandissanti, niṭṭhamettha gantabbaṁ:
root_text.an4.180:7.8: ‘addhā idaṁ tassa bhagavato vacanaṁ arahato sammāsambuddhassa;
root_text.an4.180:8.1: Idha pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhu evaṁ vadeyya:
root_text.an4.180:8.2: ‘asukasmiṁ nāma āvāse eko thero bhikkhu viharati bahussuto āgatāgamo dhammadharo vinayadharo mātikādharo.
root_text.an4.180:8.3: Tassa me therassa sammukhā sutaṁ sammukhā paṭiggahitaṁ—
root_text.an4.180:8.4: ayaṁ dhammo, ayaṁ vinayo, idaṁ satthusāsanan’ti.
root_text.an4.180:8.5: Tassa, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno bhāsitaṁ neva abhinanditabbaṁ nappaṭikkositabbaṁ.
root_text.an4.180:8.6: Anabhinanditvā appaṭikkositvā tāni padabyañjanāni sādhukaṁ uggahetvā sutte otāretabbāni, vinaye sandassetabbāni.
root_text.an4.180:8.7: Tāni ce sutte otāriyamānāni vinaye sandassiyamānāni na ceva sutte otaranti na vinaye sandissanti, niṭṭhamettha gantabbaṁ:
root_text.an4.180:8.8: ‘addhā idaṁ na ceva tassa bhagavato vacanaṁ arahato sammāsambuddhassa;
root_text.an4.180:9.1: Idha pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhu evaṁ vadeyya:
root_text.an4.180:9.2: ‘asukasmiṁ nāma āvāse eko thero bhikkhu viharati bahussuto āgatāgamo dhammadharo vinayadharo mātikādharo.
root_text.an4.180:9.3: Tassa me therassa sammukhā sutaṁ sammukhā paṭiggahitaṁ—
root_text.an4.180:9.4: ayaṁ dhammo, ayaṁ vinayo, idaṁ satthusāsanan’ti.
root_text.an4.180:9.5: Tassa, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno bhāsitaṁ neva abhinanditabbaṁ nappaṭikkositabbaṁ.
root_text.an4.180:9.6: Anabhinanditvā appaṭikkositvā tāni padabyañjanāni sādhukaṁ uggahetvā sutte otāretabbāni, vinaye sandassetabbāni.
root_text.an4.180:9.7: Tāni ce sutte otāriyamānāni vinaye sandassiyamānāni sutte ceva otaranti vinaye ca sandissanti, niṭṭhamettha gantabbaṁ:
root_text.an4.180:9.8: ‘addhā idaṁ tassa bhagavato vacanaṁ arahato sammāsambuddhassa;

translation_text.an4.180:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near the Bhoga City, at the Ānanda Shrine.
translation_text.an4.180:1.2: There the Buddha addressed the mendicants,
translation_text.an4.180:1.6: “Mendicants, I will teach you the four great references.
translation_text.an4.180:1.7: Listen and apply your mind well, I will speak.”
translation_text.an4.180:2.1: “Mendicants, what are the four great references?
translation_text.an4.180:2.3: ‘Reverend, I have heard and learned this in the presence of the Buddha:
translation_text.an4.180:2.4: this is the teaching, this is the training, this is the Teacher’s instruction.’
translation_text.an4.180:2.5: You should neither approve nor reject that mendicant’s statement.
translation_text.an4.180:2.6: Instead, having carefully memorized those words and phrases, you should make sure they fit in the discourse and are exhibited in the training.
translation_text.an4.180:2.7: If they do not fit in the discourse and are not exhibited in the training, you should draw the conclusion:
translation_text.an4.180:2.8: ‘Clearly this is not the word of the Blessed One, the perfected one, the fully awakened Buddha.
translation_text.an4.180:2.9: It has been incorrectly memorized by that mendicant.’
translation_text.an4.180:2.10: And so you should discard it.
translation_text.an4.180:3.2: ‘Reverend, I have heard and learned this in the presence of the Buddha:
translation_text.an4.180:3.3: this is the teaching, this is the training, this is the Teacher’s instruction.’
translation_text.an4.180:3.4: You should neither approve nor reject that mendicant’s statement.
translation_text.an4.180:3.5: Instead, having carefully memorized those words and phrases, you should make sure they fit in the discourse and are exhibited in the training.
translation_text.an4.180:3.6: If they fit in the discourse and are exhibited in the training, you should draw the conclusion:
translation_text.an4.180:3.7: ‘Clearly this is the word of the Blessed One, the perfected one, the fully awakened Buddha.
translation_text.an4.180:3.8: It has been correctly memorized by that mendicant.’
translation_text.an4.180:3.9: You should remember it. This is the first great reference.
translation_text.an4.180:4.2: ‘In such-and-such monastery lives a Saṅgha with seniors and leaders.
translation_text.an4.180:4.3: I’ve heard and learned this in the presence of that Saṅgha:
translation_text.an4.180:4.4: this is the teaching, this is the training, this is the Teacher’s instruction.’
translation_text.an4.180:4.5: You should neither approve nor reject that mendicant’s statement.
translation_text.an4.180:4.6: Instead, having carefully memorized those words and phrases, you should make sure they fit in the discourse and are exhibited in the training.
translation_text.an4.180:4.7: If they do not fit in the discourse and are not exhibited in the training, you should draw the conclusion:
translation_text.an4.180:4.8: ‘Clearly this is not the word of the Blessed One, the perfected one, the fully awakened Buddha.
translation_text.an4.180:4.9: It has been incorrectly memorized by that Saṅgha.’
translation_text.an4.180:4.10: And so you should discard it.
translation_text.an4.180:5.2: ‘In such-and-such monastery lives a Saṅgha with seniors and leaders.
translation_text.an4.180:5.3: I’ve heard and learned this in the presence of that Saṅgha:
translation_text.an4.180:5.4: this is the teaching, this is the training, this is the Teacher’s instruction.’
translation_text.an4.180:5.5: You should neither approve nor reject that mendicant’s statement.
translation_text.an4.180:5.6: Instead, having carefully memorized those words and phrases, you should make sure they fit in the discourse and are exhibited in the training.
translation_text.an4.180:5.7: If they fit in the discourse and are exhibited in the training, you should draw the conclusion:
translation_text.an4.180:5.8: ‘Clearly this is the word of the Blessed One, the perfected one, the fully awakened Buddha.
translation_text.an4.180:5.9: It has been correctly memorized by that Saṅgha.’
translation_text.an4.180:5.10: You should remember it. This is the second great reference.
translation_text.an4.180:6.2: ‘In such-and-such monastery there are several senior mendicants who are very learned, inheritors of the heritage, who remember the teachings, the monastic law, and the outlines.
translation_text.an4.180:6.3: I’ve heard and learned this in the presence of those senior mendicants:
translation_text.an4.180:6.4: this is the teaching, this is the training, this is the Teacher’s instruction.’
translation_text.an4.180:6.5: You should neither approve nor reject that mendicant’s statement.
translation_text.an4.180:6.6: Instead, having carefully memorized those words and phrases, you should make sure they fit in the discourse and are exhibited in the training.
translation_text.an4.180:6.7: If they do not fit in the discourse and are not exhibited in the training, you should draw the conclusion:
translation_text.an4.180:6.8: ‘Clearly this is not the word of the Blessed One, the perfected one, the fully awakened Buddha.
translation_text.an4.180:6.9: It has been incorrectly memorized by those senior mendicants.’
translation_text.an4.180:6.10: And so you should discard it.
translation_text.an4.180:7.2: ‘In such-and-such monastery there are several senior mendicants who are very learned, inheritors of the heritage, who remember the teachings, the monastic law, and the outlines.
translation_text.an4.180:7.3: I’ve heard and learned this in the presence of those senior mendicants:
translation_text.an4.180:7.4: this is the teaching, this is the monastic law, this is the Teacher’s instruction.’
translation_text.an4.180:7.5: You should neither approve nor reject that mendicant’s statement.
translation_text.an4.180:7.6: Instead, having carefully memorized those words and phrases, you should make sure they fit in the discourse and are exhibited in the training.
translation_text.an4.180:7.7: If they fit in the discourse and are exhibited in the training, you should draw the conclusion:
translation_text.an4.180:7.8: ‘Clearly this is the word of the Blessed One, the perfected one, the fully awakened Buddha.
translation_text.an4.180:7.9: It has been correctly memorized by those senior mendicants.’
translation_text.an4.180:7.10: You should remember it. This is the third great reference.
translation_text.an4.180:8.2: ‘In such-and-such monastery there is a single senior mendicant who is very learned, an inheritor of the heritage, who has memorized the teachings, the monastic law, and the outlines.
translation_text.an4.180:8.3: I’ve heard and learned this in the presence of that senior mendicant:
translation_text.an4.180:8.4: this is the teaching, this is the training, this is the Teacher’s instruction.’
translation_text.an4.180:8.5: You should neither approve nor reject that mendicant’s statement.
translation_text.an4.180:8.6: Instead, having carefully memorized those words and phrases, you should make sure they fit in the discourse and are exhibited in the training.
translation_text.an4.180:8.7: If they do not fit in the discourse and are not exhibited in the training, you should draw the conclusion:
translation_text.an4.180:8.8: ‘Clearly this is not the word of the Blessed One, the perfected one, the fully awakened Buddha.
translation_text.an4.180:8.9: It has been incorrectly memorized by that senior mendicant.’
translation_text.an4.180:8.10: And so you should discard it.
translation_text.an4.180:9.2: ‘In such-and-such monastery there is a single senior mendicant who is very learned, an inheritor of the heritage, who has memorized the teachings, the monastic law, and the outlines.
translation_text.an4.180:9.3: I’ve heard and learned this in the presence of that senior mendicant:
translation_text.an4.180:9.4: this is the teaching, this is the training, this is the Teacher’s instruction.’
translation_text.an4.180:9.5: You should neither approve nor reject that mendicant’s statement.
translation_text.an4.180:9.6: Instead, having carefully memorized those words and phrases, you should make sure they fit in the discourse and are exhibited in the training.
translation_text.an4.180:9.7: If they fit in the discourse and are exhibited in the training, you should draw the conclusion:
translation_text.an4.180:9.8: ‘Clearly this is the word of the Blessed One, the perfected one, the fully awakened Buddha.
translation_text.an4.180:9.9: It has been correctly memorized by that senior mendicant.’
translation_text.an4.180:9.10: You should remember it. This is the fourth great reference.
translation_text.an4.180:9.11: These are the four great references.”

variant_text.an4.180:2.10: hetaṁ → iti idaṁ (bj); iti hīdaṁ (sya-all, km, mr)
variant_text.an4.180:3.1: Idha pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhu evaṁ vadeyya → ettako pāṭho dīghanikāye (dn16:4.8.1) na dissati, peyyālamukhena dassito
